content: Take a screenshot and describe what you see on the macOS desktop

message.content: Take a screenshot and describe what you see on the macOS desktop

content.text:
Here's what I see on the macOS desktop:

**System Dialog:** A macOS permission prompt is displayed in the center of the screen. It says **"bash" is requesting to bypass the system private window picker and directly access your screen and audio.** It warns that this will allow bash to record the screen and system audio, including personal or sensitive information. There are two buttons: **"Allow"** (blue) and **"Open System Settings"**.

**Menu Bar:** The top of the screen shows the standard macOS menu bar with the Apple logo, **Finder** menu items (File, Edit, View, Go, Window, Help), and on the right side the date/time reads **Tue Mar 3, 6:03 AM**, along with search and control center icons.

**Dock:** The bottom dock contains a full row of application icons including Finder, Launchpad, Safari, Messages, Mail, App Store, Calendar, Contacts, Notes, FaceTime, Apple TV, Music, News, and others.

**Desktop:** The background is a dark gradient (dark gray/black), with no files or folders visible on the desktop itself.
